items.title: The Courage To Be Wrong — Dr Loshi Rajen
items.summary: The task is not simply to identify psychiatric symptoms. Sometimes, the task is to determine why those symptoms exist. The post The Courage To Be Wrong — Dr Loshi Rajen appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: NGOs Question MOH Data On Enforcement Against Online Vape Ads, Sales
items.summary: Forty-nine NGOs question the transparency of data on MOH's enforcement under Act 852, asking whether the compounds, investigation papers, and prosecutions truly reflect action against online vape advertising and sales, as they demand specific statistics. The post NGOs Question MOH Data On Enforcement Against Online Vape Ads, Sales appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Protecting Malaysia’s Children From An Invisible Threat — Dr Musa Mohd Nordin & Dr Zulkifli Ismail
items.summary: RSV is the leading cause of hospitalisation for all infants in Malaysia, yet it remains under-discussed and unaddressed. The post Protecting Malaysia’s Children From An Invisible Threat — Dr Musa Mohd Nordin & Dr Zulkifli Ismail appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Put Liquid Nicotine On Poisons List, Revisit Tobacco GEG — LCNM
items.summary: Liquid nicotine must be relisted on the Poisons List. The government should also revisit the tobacco GEG. For all the truly remarkable recent advances in the diagnosis and treatment of lung cancer, effective tobacco control remains a powerful strategy. The post Put Liquid Nicotine On Poisons List, Revisit Tobacco GEG — LCNM appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Improving Road Safety Management In Malaysia — Dr Krishnan Rajam & Dr Amar-Singh HSS
items.summary: Transforming Malaysia’s road safety landscape requires moving away from viewing traffic crashes as unpredictable accidents and instead managing them as highly predictable, preventable injuries. The post Improving Road Safety Management In Malaysia — Dr Krishnan Rajam & Dr Amar-Singh HSS appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysia Mulls Mandatory National Insurance For Long-Term Care
items.summary: Malaysia is studying the establishment of a national insurance scheme for long-term care like Japan and Singapore, with potentially mandatory contributions of RM100 monthly base premiums to fund aged care. A pilot may begin in 2027 with civil servants. The post Malaysia Mulls Mandatory National Insurance For Long-Term Care appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Rethinking Distributive Justice In Malaysian Health Care — Erwin Khoo Jiayuan
items.summary: The real scandal is not that we are debating how to increase health care capacity in the Klang Valley, but that our national health expenditure as a percentage of the country's GDP remains stagnant despite clear, documented needs across every state. The post Rethinking Distributive Justice In Malaysian Health Care — Erwin Khoo Jiayuan appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: New PJ Hospital, Leased Lifeline, Or A Question We Dare Not Ask? — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam
items.summary: PJ residents have waited a generation for a hospital. They can wait a little longer for the right decision. What they can't afford is a monument to political ambition that stands empty for years or a building that siphons staff from surrounding facilities. The post New PJ Hospital, Leased Lifeline, Or A Question We Dare Not Ask? — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Someone Still Has To Stay: Limiting Housemen Hours Comes At A Cost — Medical Officer
items.summary: I'm not against improving HO welfare, but why is MO welfare always ignored? If fatigue is dangerous for HOs, then fatigue is also dangerous for MOs. If burnout matters for HOs, then burnout also matters for MOs. MOs hold up the health care system. The post Someone Still Has To Stay: Limiting Housemen Hours Comes At A Cost — Medical Officer appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: PJ Hospital Project Needs Land Acquisition, Road Upgrades, Says Taman Medan Rep
items.summary: Taman Medan rep Dr Afif Bahardin says the proposed PJ Hospital site in his constituency needs acquisition of the private land and major road upgrades for the jam-packed area. He suggests two alternative sites of government-owned land: UiTM or UIA in PJ. The post PJ Hospital Project Needs Land Acquisition, Road Upgrades, Says Taman Medan Rep appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Lab assistant facing murder charge over Klang delivery rider’s death pleads not guilty to drug offence
items.summary: KLANG, June 3 — A laboratory assistant who was previously charged with killing a motorcyclist in a road...
